examples.title: Proportional Frame Allocation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to allocate frames to processes proportionally based on their size
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ProportionalFrameAllocation
INPUT: List of processes P, total_free_frames F
OUTPUT: Frames allocated to each process

BEGIN
  total_size ← 0
  FOR each process p IN P DO
    total_size ← total_size + p.size
  END FOR

  FOR each process p IN P DO
    proportion ← p.size / total_size
    p.allocated_frames ← proportion × F
  END FOR

  remaining_frames ← F - sum(p.allocated_frames) for all p
  Distribute remaining_frames using round-robin
  RETURN allocation
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates each process's proportion of total memory size and allocates frames accordingly. The larger the process, the more frames it receives. Any remaining frames are distributed round-robin.

examples.title: Page Fault Frequency Control Algorithm
examples.description: Dynamic frame allocation based on monitoring page fault rates
examples.explanation: This implementation allocates minimum frames to all processes, then adjusts allocations based on observed page fault frequency. Processes with higher fault rates get more frames.

examples.title: Fixed Frame Allocation Implementation
examples.description: Simple fixed allocation where each process gets equal number of frames
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates fixed frame allocation where all processes receive an equal number of frames (100/3 = 33 each with 1 remaining). Simple but may cause inefficiency if process sizes vary greatly.

examples.title: Priority-Based Frame Allocation
examples.description: Frame allocation that considers process priority alongside size
examples.explanation: This Java implementation allocates frames proportionally by size first, then adds priority bonuses. Higher priority processes receive additional frames beyond their proportional share, ensuring critical processes have more memory available.
